(born **Sharon Forsmoe**, December 24, 1940 – May 15, 2023) was an American actress and dancer. Originally beginning her career as a ballerina with the [American Ballet Theatre](/wiki/American_Ballet_Theatre "American Ballet Theatre") company, Farrell made her film debut in 1959 in *Kiss Her Goodbye*, followed by roles in *[40 Pounds of Trouble](/wiki/40_Pounds_of_Trouble "40 Pounds of Trouble")* (1962), and the [neo-noirs](/wiki/Neo-noir "Neo-noir") *[A Lovely Way to Die](/wiki/A_Lovely_Way_to_Die "A Lovely Way to Die")* (1968) and *[Marlowe](/wiki/Marlowe_(1969_film) "Marlowe (1969 film)")* (1969). She worked prolifically in television, including recurring parts in the series *[Saints and Sinners](/wiki/Saints_and_Sinners_(1962_TV_series) "Saints and Sinners (1962 TV series)")* (1962), *[Dr. Kildare](/wiki/Dr._Kildare_(TV_series) "Dr. Kildare (TV series)")* (1965), and *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* (1980).

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f2/Sharon_Farrell_Saints_and_Sinners_1962.jpg/250px-Sharon_Farrell_Saints_and_Sinners_1962.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sharon_Farrell_Saints_and_Sinners_1962.jpg)

On TV's *[Saints and Sinners](/wiki/Saints_and_Sinners_(1962_TV_series) "Saints and Sinners (1962 TV series)")* (1962)

**Sharon Farrell** (born **Sharon Forsmoe**, December 24, 1940 – May 15, 2023) was an American actress and dancer. Originally beginning her career as a ballerina with the [American Ballet Theatre](/wiki/American_Ballet_Theatre "American Ballet Theatre") company, Farrell made her film debut in 1959 in *Kiss Her Goodbye*, followed by roles in *[40 Pounds of Trouble](/wiki/40_Pounds_of_Trouble "40 Pounds of Trouble")* (1962), and the [neo-noirs](/wiki/Neo-noir "Neo-noir") *[A Lovely Way to Die](/wiki/A_Lovely_Way_to_Die "A Lovely Way to Die")* (1968) and *[Marlowe](/wiki/Marlowe_(1969_film) "Marlowe (1969 film)")* (1969). She worked prolifically in television, including recurring parts in the series *[Saints and Sinners](/wiki/Saints_and_Sinners_(1962_TV_series) "Saints and Sinners (1962 TV series)")* (1962), *[Dr. Kildare](/wiki/Dr._Kildare_(TV_series) "Dr. Kildare (TV series)")* (1965), and *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* (1980).

Farrell's other roles include [Larry Cohen](/wiki/Larry_Cohen "Larry Cohen")'s horror film *[It's Alive](/wiki/It%27s_Alive_(1974_film) "It's Alive (1974 film)")* (1974), [Dennis Hopper](/wiki/Dennis_Hopper "Dennis Hopper")'s drama film *[Out of the Blue](/wiki/Out_of_the_Blue_(1980_film) "Out of the Blue (1980 film)")* (1980), and the teen comedy *[Can't Buy Me Love](/wiki/Can%27t_Buy_Me_Love_(film) "Can't Buy Me Love (film)")* (1987). She continued to appear in television and film until 1999. In 2013, she reappeared in a minor role in the [web series](/wiki/Web_series "Web series") *Broken at Love*. Until her death in 2023, she was the last living [*Hawaii Five-O*](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)") cast member.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sharon_Farrell&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Born Sharon Forsmoe on Christmas Eve 1940[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEStrodder2007324-2)
to Hazel Ruth[[1]](#cite_note-waterburyfunerals/hazel-forsmoe-1)[[3]](#cite_note-legacy/188729801-3) (née Huffman) and Darrel LaValle Forsmoe[[4]](#cite_note-ancientfaces/157534946-4)[[5]](#cite_note-ancestry/Census-Forsmoe_4bx2tp-5)[[6]](#cite_note-siouxcityjournal/hazel-r-e-forsmoe-6) in Sioux City, Iowa,[[7]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFarrell2014xii-7)[[8]](#cite_note-sioux-8) she was of Norwegian descent[[9]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFarrell20144-9) and was raised with her sister, Dale Candice, in a Lutheran family.[[10]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFarrell20145-10) During her childhood, Farrell studied ballet and was involved in the theater department during high school.[[11]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFarrell20146-11) Farrell toured with the [American Ballet Theatre](/wiki/American_Ballet_Theatre "American Ballet Theatre") Company as a dancer, which brought her to New York City.[[12]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFarrell201421-12)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sharon_Farrell&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Farrell made her acting debut at age 18 in the 1959 film *Kiss Her Goodbye*. She took her stage name from combining her surname and her father's given name Darrel, with "F" for Forsmoe and two "L"s.[[13]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFarrell201462-13)

From the 1960s to the 1980s, Farrell appeared in such films as *[The Reivers](/wiki/The_Reivers_(film) "The Reivers (film)")* (1969), *[Marlowe](/wiki/Marlowe_(1969_film) "Marlowe (1969 film)")* (1969), *[It's Alive](/wiki/It%27s_Alive_(1974_film) "It's Alive (1974 film)")* (1974), *[The Stunt Man](/wiki/The_Stunt_Man "The Stunt Man")* (1980), *[Out of the Blue](/wiki/Out_of_the_Blue_(1980_film) "Out of the Blue (1980 film)")* (1980), *[Night of the Comet](/wiki/Night_of_the_Comet "Night of the Comet")* (1984), and *[Can't Buy Me Love](/wiki/Can%27t_Buy_Me_Love_(film) "Can't Buy Me Love (film)")* (1987).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In addition to film work, Farrell also appeared in guest roles on various television shows, including *[Death Valley Days](/wiki/Death_Valley_Days "Death Valley Days")*, *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*, *[The Wild Wild West](/wiki/The_Wild_Wild_West "The Wild Wild West")*, *[The Man from U.N.C.L.E.](/wiki/The_Man_from_U.N.C.L.E. "The Man from U.N.C.L.E.")*, *[I Dream of Jeannie](/wiki/I_Dream_of_Jeannie "I Dream of Jeannie")*, *[My Favorite Martian](/wiki/My_Favorite_Martian "My Favorite Martian")*, *[The Beverly Hillbillies](/wiki/The_Beverly_Hillbillies "The Beverly Hillbillies")*, *[The Alfred Hitchcock Hour](/wiki/The_Alfred_Hitchcock_Hour "The Alfred Hitchcock Hour")*, and *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*.

In 1991, she joined the cast of the long-running soap opera *[The Young and the Restless](/wiki/The_Young_and_the_Restless "The Young and the Restless")*, remaining with the show until 1997. Farrell's last television role was in a 1999 episode of *[JAG](/wiki/JAG_(TV_series) "JAG (TV series)")*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Between 2013 and 2014, Farrell appeared in the web series *Broken at Love*, marking her first on-screen appearance in 14 years.[[14]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTETerrace201439-14)

## Personal life and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sharon_Farrell&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life and death")]

Farrell's first marriage was to actor [Andrew Prine](/wiki/Andrew_Prine "Andrew Prine")[[15]](#cite_note-sun-15) in 1962. They later divorced, reportedly after only living together for one month and ten days.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Farrell had one son, Chance Boyer, born when she was dating actor John F. Boyer. After Chance's birth in 1970, Farrell suffered an [embolism](/wiki/Embolism "Embolism"), which caused her heart to stop beating for four minutes. She incurred serious brain damage that resulted in memory loss and physical impairments. With the help of colleagues, Farrell worked to regain her abilities, including her memory. She resumed her acting career, but for many years kept her illness a secret under the advice of friend and actor [Steve McQueen](/wiki/Steve_McQueen "Steve McQueen"), who warned her that if word got out, her career would be over.[[15]](#cite_note-sun-15)[[17]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTELisantiPaul2002125-17)

Farrell was married to director Dale Trevillian for 32 years.[[15]](#cite_note-sun-15) In her autobiography *Sharon Farrell: "Hollywood Princess" from Sioux City, Iowa*, she claimed that she had romantic relationships with many famous people, including [Che Guevara](/wiki/Che_Guevara "Che Guevara"), Steve McQueen, and [Bruce Lee](/wiki/Bruce_Lee "Bruce Lee").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Unreported until 5 August 2023, Farrell died on 15 May 2023, at the age of 82, possibly in [Orange County, California](/wiki/Orange_County,_California "Orange County, California"),[[19]](#cite_note-19) or [Los Angeles Downtown Medical Center](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Downtown_Medical_Center "Los Angeles Downtown Medical Center").[[20]](#cite_note-newspressnow/sharon-farrell-dies-20)
